title: Spathodea tulipifera
taxonomy: Spathodea tulipifera is a species of tree. It belongs to the family Bignoniaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales. Within the order Lamiales, Spathodea tulipifera is classified under the class Magnoliopsida, also known as flowering plants. The kingdom it falls under is Plantae, which includes all plant species.
uses: Spathodea tulipifera, also known as the tulip tree, is a species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In West Africa, the bark of the tree is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The leaves and seeds of the tree are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the tree's wood is used to make medicine sticks that are believed to have healing properties.
